author: svlachakis
content:
Hi all, <@1345762296873619456> here from Nethermind core team, wanted to bring up some concerns that we have with eth_fillTransaction endpoint.

TLDR: eth_fillTransaction returns both a tx object and an RLP-encoded raw. There's no binding between them, a malicious RPC provider can return a tx that matches my request and a raw that encodes a completely different transaction. A caller who signs raw is signing the attacker's tx.

Example:

Call: eth_fillTransaction({from: U, to: AccountA, value: 1 ETH})

Reply:
tx: {from: U, to: AccountA, value: 1 ETH, nonce/gas/fees: …} ← matches request
raw: RLP({from: U, to: Attacker, value: drain(U), data: …}) ← unrelated

Sign raw → drained.

Proposal: Drop raw from the response, or don't expose the method at all. Callers that need RLP can encode it locally from the verified tx object in a few lines, that's the only safe path anyway.

The filled-by-RPC fields (nonce, gasLimit, fees) are still trusted in the tx-object form, but the worst case there is failure / overpayment, not funds-to-attacker. tx-only is strictly safer than tx + raw.

I think it's a topic that should be included on our next RPC Standards call.

cc: <@758579010027782144>

author: svlachakis
content:
One more topic for next RPC Standards call should be this issue (it's updated as we go) https://github.com/NethermindEth/nethermind/issues/11412. 

We have noticed a few discrepancies between hive tests generated by geth compared to what the endpoint specs expect for a few endpoints.

author: mysticryuujin
content: The fixtures get generated by geth, so probably we need PRs to fix geth

author: boma_naps
content: Strong agree on dropping raw  it's just rlp(tx), so callers lose nothing by computing it locally, and keeping it widens the gap between what wallets display and what they actually sign. Worth putting on the next standards call agenda.

author: boma_naps
content:
This is an error mapper thingy it
  sends ErrFeeCapTooLow straight to -32602 instead of the spec's -38012, but is there way to relax the spec rather than just track the drift?

author: boma_naps
content: I will make a pr on the spec because  error messages are pinned to exact strings even though only the code carries meaning and also debug_getRawTransaction doesn't define what happens with a malformed hash then we can discuss further on the call?

author: svlachakis
content: concerning debug_getRawTransaction I think the issue is that there needs to be a generic error message not a go specific one.

author: boma_naps
content: Yeah messages should be  free-form, and the matcher should check codes only.

author: s1na
content:
I think the method still makes sense. It is a helper to fill out a tx as we don't officially support sendTransaction anymore and only sendRawTransaction. Specially with the blob txes. The node is already well position to compute blob proofs etc for you.

I can get behind removing the rlp encoded tx! While generally current rpc is supposed to be trusted (e.g. can return totally wrong logs or eth balance to users), the "write" methods are even riskier as you point out.

author: svlachakis
content: concerning ethSimulate looks like there are a lot of discrepencies, I think both geth needs fixing and spec needs improvements on codes and messages because it's not clear in a few cases

author: boma_naps
content: I will look into this also and make a pr
